name: golang-github-julienschmidt-httprouter-dev
title: golang-github-julienschmidt-httprouter-dev
summary: High performance HTTP request router for Go that scales well
description:
High performance HTTP request router for Go that scales well
HttpRouter (github.com/julienschmidt/httprouter) is a lightweight high
performance HTTP request router (also called multiplexer or just mux for
short) for Go.
.
In contrast to the default mux of Go's net/http package, this router supports
variables in the routing pattern and matches against the request method. It
also scales better.
.
The router is optimized for high performance and a small memory footprint. It
scales well even with very long paths and a large number of routes. A
compressing dynamic trie (radix tree) structure is used for efficient
matching.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-julienschmidt-httprouter-dev because High performance HTTP request router for Go that scales well.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-julienschmidt-httprouter-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-julienschmidt-httprouter-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-julienschmidt-httprouter-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
